examples.english: I am beyond excited that we've won the championship.
examples.pronunciation: yuushou dekite, kangeki no kiwami desu.

examples.japanese: 長時間の試合で、選手たちは疲労の極みに達していた。
examples.english: The players were completely exhausted during the long-lasting game.
examples.pronunciation: choujikan no shiai de, senshu tachi wa hirou no kiwami ni tasshite ita.

examples.japanese: 交通事故で多くの人が亡くなり、痛恨の極みだ。
examples.english: Many people lose their lives in traffic accidents, it is very tragic.
examples.pronunciation: koutsuu jiko de ooku no hito ga nakunari, tsuukon no kiwami da.

examples.japanese: 家を建てたばかりなのに、地震で壊れてしまい、痛恨の極みだ。
examples.english: Our house was destroyed in the earthquake immediately after we finished building it. It is such a horrible feeling.
examples.pronunciation: ie o tateta bakari na noni, jishin de kowarete shimai, tsuukon no kiwami da.

examples.japanese: 私のような者がこのような名誉ある賞を頂けるとは、感激の極みだ。
examples.english: I'm deeply honored that someone like me to receive such an honorable award.
examples.pronunciation: watashi no youna mono ga ko no youna meiyo aru shou o itadakeru to wa, kangeki no kiwami da.

examples.japanese: あなたにとっては日常的なことかもしれませんが、私にとってはそれは贅沢の極みです。
examples.english: It may be everyday for you, but for me it's the height of luxury.
examples.pronunciation: anata ni totte wa nichijouteki na koto kamo shiremasen ga, watashi ni totte wa sore wa zeitaku no kiwami desu.

examples.japanese: このような盛大なる激励会を開いていただき、感激の極みです。
examples.english: We are very grateful that you have hosted such a great encouragement party like this.
examples.pronunciation: kono youna seidai naru gekireikai o aite itadaki, kangeki no kiwami desu.

meaning: utmost; extremely ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Nの + 至り
usageJapanese:
最高に〜だ。
程度が極めて高く、最高の状態に達していることを表す。
usageNotes: かなり硬い表現。改まった場面で使われる。

examples.japanese: こんな素晴らしい賞をいただいて、光栄の至りです。
examples.english: It is an honor to receive such a wonderful award.
examples.pronunciation: konna subarashii shou o itadaite, kouei no itari desu.

examples.japanese: 憧れのアーティストからサインをもらえるなんて、感激の至りだ。
examples.english: I was deeply moved because I got a signature from the artist I admired.
examples.pronunciation: akogareno aatisuto kara sain o moraeru nante, kangeki no itari da.

examples.japanese: そんなことをするのは愚の至りだ。
examples.english: Doing something like that is the height of foolishness.
examples.pronunciation: sonna koto o suru no wa gu no itari da.

examples.japanese: 日本の首相にお目にかかって、私は光栄の至りです。
examples.english: It is such an honor for me to meet the Prime Minister of Japan.
examples.pronunciation: nihon no shushou ni ome ni kakatte, watashi wa kouei no itari desu.

examples.japanese: 今日はお客様の名前を間違えてしまい、汗顔の至りだ。
examples.english: Today, I made a mistake with a customer's name, and so my face is covered in sweat.
examples.pronunciation: kyou wa okyakusama no namae o machigaete shimai, kangan no itari da.

examples.japanese: 自分の書いた物語が本になるなど想像していませんでした。光栄の至りです。
examples.english: I never imagined that the story I wrote would become a book. It's such an honor.
examples.pronunciation: jibun no kaita monogatari ga hon ni naru nado souzou shite imasen deshita. kouei no itari desu.

examples.japanese: 私の研究はそのような評価を受けたということは感激の至りです。
examples.english: It is such an honor that my research received such an evaluation.
examples.pronunciation: watashi no kenkyuu wa sono youna hyouka o uketa toiu koto wa kangeki no itari desu.

meaning: let alone; not to mention; needless to say ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
V（辞書形）+　のはおろか
N + はおろか
usageJapanese: 〜は当然として
usageNotes:
①後ろはマイナスのことがくる。そのため否定形が多い。
②硬い表現
③「〜さえ・〜までも、〜も」といった言葉と一緒によく使われる。

examples.japanese: 僕の地元はモールはおろかコンビニすらない。
examples.english: My hometown doesn't even have a convenience store, let alone a mall.
examples.pronunciation: boku no jimoto wa mooru wa oro ka konbini sura nai.

examples.japanese: 納豆は嫌いなので、食べることはおろか、匂いを嗅ぐこともしたくない。
examples.english: I hate natto, so I don't want to smell it, let alone eat it.
examples.pronunciation: nattou wa kirai na node, taberu koto wa oro ka, nioi o kagu koto mo shitakunai.

examples.japanese: 彼は敬語はおろか、簡単な日本語さえできない。
examples.english: He can't even speak simple Japanese, let alone honorifics.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa keigo wa oro ka, kantan na nihongo sae dekinai.

examples.japanese: 彼は自分の会社はおろか住んでいる家まで失ってしまった。
examples.english: He even lost the house he lived in, not to mention his company.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa jibun no kaisha wa oro ka sunde iru ie made ushinatte shimatta.

examples.japanese: 事故で怪我をして、歩くのはおろか立つことすらできない。
examples.english: I was injured in an accident and can't even stand, let alone walk.
examples.pronunciation: jiko de kega o shite, aruku no wa oro ka tatsu koto sura dekinai.

examples.japanese: 私の国の経済は不景気で、正社員はおろかアルバイトすら見つからない。
examples.english: The economy of my country is in recession, and I can't even find a part-time job, let alone a full-time one.
examples.pronunciation: watashi no kuni no keizai wa fukeiki de, seishain wa oro ka arubaito sura mitsukaranai.

examples.japanese: この道は通行が禁止されています。車はおろか、人も通ることができません。
examples.english: This road is closed to traffic. No one can pass, let alone cars.
examples.pronunciation: kono michi wa tsuukou ga kinshi sarete imasu. kuruma wa oroka, hito mo tooru koto ga dekimasen.

examples.japanese: 事故で記憶を失った彼女は、住所はおろか、名前すら覚えていなかった。
examples.english: She lost her memory in the accident and didn't even remember her name, let alone her address.
examples.pronunciation: jiko de kioku o ushinatta kanojo wa, juusho wa oro ka, namae sura oboete inakatta.

meaning: setting aside ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: N + はさておき
usageJapanese: 〜のことは今は置いておいて
usageNotes: 話題を変え、本題に入るときなどに使う。

examples.japanese: この本、内容はさておき、タイトルはすごくいいと思う。
examples.english: As for this book, content aside, I think the title is very good.
examples.pronunciation: kono hon, naiyou wa sateoki, taitoru wa sugoku ii to omou.

examples.japanese: 彼女の料理は見た目はさておき、味はとても美味しいよ。
examples.english: Setting aside the appearance, her food tastes very good.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo no ryouri wa mitame wa sateoki, aji wa totemo oishii yo.

examples.japanese: 冗談はさておき、この件についての解決方法を考えましょう。
examples.english: Joking aside, let's think of a solution to this matter.
examples.pronunciation: jodan wa sateoki, kono ken ni tsuite no kaiketsu houhou o kangaemashou.

examples.japanese: 難しい話はさておき、今日は食事を楽しみましょう。
examples.english: Difficult stories aside, let's enjoy our meal today.
examples.pronunciation: muzukashii hanashi wa sateoki, kyou wa shokuji o tanoshimi mashou.

examples.japanese: この話はさておき、教科書の80ページを見てください。
examples.english: Moving on, please look at page 80 in the textbook.
examples.pronunciation: kono hanashi wa sateoki, kyoukasho no 80 peeji o mite kudasai.

examples.japanese: 新しいスマホはデザインはさておき、素晴らしい機能が充実している。
examples.english: Aside from the design, the new smartphone has a lot of great features.
examples.pronunciation: atarashii sumaho wa dezain wa sateoki, subarashii kinou ga juujitsu shiteiru.

examples.japanese: お金の問題はさておき、今は病気を治すことに専念すべきだ。
examples.english: Money problem aside, now we should concentrate on curing the illness.
examples.pronunciation: okane no mondai wa sateoki, ima wa byouki o naosu koto ni sennen subeki da.

id: grammar:n1:はそっちのけで / をそっちのけで
word: はそっちのけで / をそっちのけで
meaning: ignoring (one thing) for (another)​​; instead of ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
Fixed expression / pattern: はそっちのけで
Fixed expression / pattern: をそっちのけで
connectionSourcePoint: はそっちのけで / をそっちのけで

examples.japanese: 彼は勉強そっちのけで遊んでばかりいた。
examples.english: He doesn't study at all, he just plays around.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa benkyou socchinoke de asonde bakari ita.

examples.japanese: 私の同僚は仕事をそっちのけでスマホ触ってばかりだ。
examples.english: My co-workers don't focus on work and are constantly touching their phones.
examples.pronunciation: watashi no douryou wa shigoto o socchinoke de sumaho sawatte bakari da.

examples.japanese: 妻は家事そっちのけで猫と遊んでいる。
examples.english: My wife is playing with the cat instead of doing household chores.
examples.pronunciation: tsuma wa kaji socchinoke de neko to asonde iru.

examples.japanese: 生徒たちは先生の注意をそっちのけでおしゃべりに夢中だ。
examples.english: The students ignored the teacher's warnings and were caught up in their conversations.
examples.pronunciation: seito tachi wa sensei no chuui o socchinoke de oshaberi ni muchuu da.

examples.japanese: 大学4年生の弟は就職はそっちのけで、毎日パソコンゲームに没頭している。
examples.english: My younger brother is a fourth year university student who instead of searching for a job, is absorbed in computer games every day.
examples.pronunciation: daigaku 4 nensei no otouto wa shuushoku wa socchinoke de, mainichi pasokon geemu ni bottou shite iru.

examples.japanese: 彼女は本業をそっちのけで副業に精を出している。
examples.english: She is devoting herself to her side business, leaving her main business behind.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa hongyou o socchinoke de fukugyou ni sei o dashite iru.

examples.japanese: この小説面白すぎて、いろいろそっちのけで読みふけってしまいそう。
examples.english: This novel is so interesting that I may leave many things (that I should do) aside to indulge myself in it.
examples.pronunciation: kono shousetsu omoshiro sugite, iroiro socchinoke de yomifukette shimai sou.

meaning: however; whatever​ ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: sentence / phrase + はどうであれ + sentence / phrase

examples.japanese: 天候はどうであれ、行かなくてはならない。
examples.english: Whatever the weather, I have to go.
examples.pronunciation: tenkou wa dou de are, ikanakute wa naranai.

examples.japanese: 結果はどうであれ、僕が悔いはない。
examples.english: Whatever the result, I have no regrets.
examples.pronunciation: kekka wa dou de are, boku ga kui wa nai.

examples.japanese: 君の居場所はどこであれ、必ず会いに行くから。
examples.english: Wherever you are, I'll definitely go see you.
examples.pronunciation: kimi no ibasho wa doko de are, kanarazu ai ni iku kara.

examples.japanese: あなたの意見がどうであれ、私はこれを決行します。
examples.english: Whatever your opinion, I will do this.
examples.pronunciation: anata no iken ga dou de are, watashi wa kore o kekkou shimasu.

examples.japanese: 値段はどうであれ、必要のない物を絶対買わない。
examples.english: Whatever the price, never buy something you don't need.
examples.pronunciation: nedan wa dou de are, hitsuyou no nai mono o zettai kawanai.

examples.japanese: 理由は何であれ、教室での携帯電話の使用は認めません。
examples.english: Whatever the reason, we do not allow the use of mobile phones in the classroom.
examples.pronunciation: riyuu wa nan de are, kyoushitsu de no keitai denwa no shiyou wa mitomemasen.

examples.japanese: あなたの過去はどうであれ、あなたを支えて行きたい。
examples.english: I want to support you no matter what your past is.
examples.pronunciation: anata no kako wa dou de are, anata o sasaete ikitai.

meaning: only because ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: V（ば形） + ばこそ

examples.japanese: 優勝できたのは、チーム全員の協力あればこそだ。
examples.english: We could only win because of our teamwork.
examples.pronunciation: yuushou dekita nowa chiimu zenin no kyouryoku areba koso da.

examples.japanese: 家族を愛すればこそ、自分が犠牲になることなどは恐れない。
examples.english: Because I love my family, I’m not afraid to sacrifice myself.
examples.pronunciation: kazoku o aisureba koso, jibun ga gisei ni naru koto nado wa osorenai.

examples.japanese: 子供のためを思えばこそ、費用は子ども自身に用意させたのです。
examples.english: I made my children pay for their own expenses because I think it’s good for them.
examples.pronunciation: kodomo no tame o omoeba koso, hiyou wa kodomo jishin ni youi saseta no desu.

examples.japanese: 自分の努力を認めてくれる人がいればこそ、やる気も出てくるというものだ。
examples.english: Only if there are people who acknowledge their efforts will they be motivated.
examples.pronunciation: jibun no doryoku o mitomete kureru hito ga ireba koso, yaruki mo detekuru toiu mono da.

examples.japanese: 忙しければこそ、時間の使い方が上手になってくるものだ。
examples.english: The more busy you are, the better you will be at using your time.
examples.pronunciation: isogashikereba koso, jikan no tsukaikata ga jouzu ni natte kuru mono da.

examples.japanese: 夜中に一人で歩いて帰れるのも、この街が安全であればこそだ。
examples.english: You can walk home alone in the middle of the night only if the city is safe.
examples.pronunciation: yonaka ni hitori de aruite kaereru no mo, kono machi ga anzen deareba koso da.

examples.japanese: 娘の将来を思えばこそ、彼との結婚には賛成できない。
examples.english: I can't agree to marry him because of my daughter's future.
examples.pronunciation: musume no shourai o omoeba koso, kare to no kekkon ni wa sansei dekinai.

id: grammar:n1:ばそれまでだ / たらそれまでだ
word: ばそれまでだ / たらそれまでだ
meaning: if… then it’s over
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
V（ば形） + ばそれまでだ
V（た形） + たらそれまでだ
connectionSourcePoint: ばそれまでだ / たらそれまでだ

examples.japanese: いくらお金をためでも、死んでしまえばそれまでだ。
examples.english: Even if you make a lot of money, it's pointless if you die.
examples.pronunciation: ikura okane o tame demo, shinde shimaeba sore made da.

examples.japanese: どんなに大変でも諦めたらそれまでだ。
examples.english: No matter how hard it may be, once you give up, it's all over.
examples.pronunciation: donnani taihen demo akirametara sore made da.

examples.japanese: いくら参考書を買っても、使わなければそれまでだ。
examples.english: No matter how many reference books you buy, if you don't use them they are meaningless.
examples.pronunciation: ikura sankousho o katte mo, tsukawanakereba sore made da.

examples.japanese: 一生懸命勉強しても、試験の日に風邪を引いたらそれまでだ。
examples.english: Even if you study hard, if you catch a cold on the day of the exam, then it's over.
examples.pronunciation: isshoukenmei benkyou shite mo, shiken no hi ni kaze o hiitara sore made da.

examples.japanese: ハードディスクに写真を保存しておいても、ハードディクスが壊れてしまえばそれまでだ。
examples.english: Even if you save your photos on a hard disk, they will all be gone if the hard disc breaks.
examples.pronunciation: haadodisuku ni shashin o hozon shite oite mo, haadodisuku ga kowarete shimaeba sore made da.

examples.japanese: 仕事も大事だが、何よりも健康が第一だ。病気になってしまったらそれまでだ。
examples.english: Work is important, but health is the number one priority. Once you get sick, it's over.
examples.pronunciation: shigoto mo daiji da ga, nani yori mo kenkou ga daiichi da. byouki ni natte shimattara sore made da.

examples.japanese: どんなに美しい花でも、散ってしまえばそれまでだ。
examples.english: No matter how beautiful a flower may be, when they start to scatter it’s over.
examples.pronunciation: donna ni utsukushii hana demo, chitte shimaeba sore made da.

id: grammar:n1:びる / びて / びた
word: びる / びて / びた
meaning: to seem to be; to appear; to behave as ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
Fixed expression / pattern: びる
Fixed expression / pattern: びて
Fixed expression / pattern: びた
connectionSourcePoint: びる / びて / びた

examples.japanese: え、彼女３０歳なの？ずいぶん幼びて見えますね。
examples.english: What, she is 30!? She looks very young.
examples.pronunciation: e, kanojo 30 sai nano? zuibun osana bite miemasu ne.

examples.japanese: 彼はカバンから古びたカメラを取り出した。
examples.english: He took an ancient‐looking camera out of his bag.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa kaban kara furu bita kamera o toridashita.

examples.japanese: 姪っ子はまだ13歳だけど、最近大人びてきた。
examples.english: My niece is only 13 years old, but recently she is starting to seem like an adult.
examples.pronunciation: meikko wa mada 13 sai da kedo, saikin otona bite kita.

examples.japanese: 息子は中学生になったとたん、大人びたことを言うようになった。
examples.english: After my son became a middle school student, he started talking like an adult.
examples.pronunciation: musuko wa chuugakusei ni natta totan, otona bita koto o iu you ni natta.

examples.japanese: 彼女はずいぶん幼びて見えますね。未成年じゃないの？
examples.english: She looks very young. Isn't she a minor?
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa zuibun you bite miemasu ne. miseinen janai no?

examples.japanese: 老後は田舎びた街でゆっくりと静かに暮らしたいと思っています。
examples.english: In old age, I want to live slowly and quietly in a rural town.
examples.pronunciation: rougo wa inaka bita machi de yukkuri to shizuka ni kurashitai to omotte imasu.

examples.japanese: 母はずっとその古びた自転車を大事に使っている。父からの誕生日プレゼントだから。
examples.english: My mother always uses that old looking bicycle with great care. That's because it was a birthday present from my Dad.
examples.pronunciation: haha wa zutto sono furu bita jitensha o daiji ni tsukatte iru. chichi kara no tanjoubi purezento da kara.

meaning: style; manner; way​
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
Fixed expression / pattern: ぶり
Fixed expression / pattern: っぷり

examples.japanese: 先生はその生徒のがんばりぶりを見ていた。
examples.english: The teacher has been observing how hard that student has been trying.
examples.pronunciation: sensei wa sono seito no ganbari buri o mite ita.

examples.japanese: 社長の話しぶり、今年のバーナスはないようだ。
examples.english: From the way the director talks, there will probably be no bonus this year.
examples.pronunciation: shachou no hanashi buri dewa, kotoshi no boonasu wa nai you da.

examples.japanese: 先輩の仕事ぶりを見ながら要領を覚えよう。
examples.english: Please watch how seniors work and remember the operations.
examples.pronunciation: senpai no shigoto buri o mi nagara youryou o oboeyou.

examples.japanese: 彼の生活ぶりを見ていると、将来が心配になる。
examples.english: Looking at his life, I'm worried about his future.
examples.pronunciation: kare no seikatsu buri o mite iru to, shourai ga shinpai ni naru.

examples.japanese: 父は、英語で話しかけられただけですごい慌てぶりだった。
examples.english: My father got all panicked just because someone spoke to him in English.
examples.pronunciation: chichi wa, eigo de hanashi kakerareta dake de sugoi awate buri datta.

examples.japanese: 娘からの手紙で日本での暮らしぶりがよくわかった。
examples.english: From a letter my daughter wrote me, I understand how she lives in Japan pretty well.
examples.pronunciation: musume kara no tegami de nihon de no kurashi buri ga yoku wakatta.

examples.japanese: 選手の活躍ぶりがテレビで紹介されていた。
examples.english: The athlete’s great efforts were featured on television.
examples.pronunciation: senshu no katsuyaku buri ga terebi de shoukai sarete ita.

examples.japanese: 彼女の話しっぷりからお父さんの体調があまりよくないことが想像できた。
examples.english: I could tell from the way she talked that her father was not in such good shape.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo no hanashippuri kara otousan no taichou ga amari yokunai koto ga souzou dekita.

id: grammar:n1:ぶる / ぶって / ぶった
word: ぶる / ぶって / ぶった
meaning: assuming the air of; behaving like; to pretend / act like ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
Fixed expression / pattern: ぶる
Fixed expression / pattern: ぶって
Fixed expression / pattern: ぶった
connectionSourcePoint: ぶる / ぶって / ぶった

examples.japanese: 彼は金持ちぶっているが、本当は借金がたくさんある。
examples.english: Though he pretends to be rich, in reality he is in serious debt.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa kanemochi butte iru ga, hontou wa shakkin ga takusan aru.

examples.japanese: 親切ぶって近づいてくる人には気を付けてください。
examples.english: Be careful of people who approach you and pretend to be nice.
examples.pronunciation: shinsetsu butte chikadzuite kuru hito ni wa ki o tsukete kudasai.

examples.japanese: まだまだ子供のくせに大人ぶっちゃって、どうしたの？
examples.english: Even though you're still a child, you're pretending to be an adult. What's wrong?
examples.pronunciation: mada mada kodomo no kuse ni otona bucchatte, doushita no?

examples.japanese: 若者ぶって薄着などするから風邪をひくんですよ。
examples.english: You keep acting like you're still young and wearing thin clothes so that's why you catch a cold.
examples.pronunciation: wakamono butte usugi nado suru kara kaze o hikun desu yo.

examples.japanese: 息子は悪ぶっているが、実は気の弱い優しい子です。
examples.english: My son acts tough, but he is actually quite weak and gentle.
examples.pronunciation: musuko wa waru butte iru ga, jitsu wa ki no yowai yasashii ko desu.

examples.japanese: 姉はみんなの前では、いつも上品ぶっているけど、家ではそうでもありませんよ。
examples.english: My sister is always classy in front of everyone, but not at home.
examples.pronunciation: ane wa minna no maede wa, itsumo jouhin butte iru kedo, ie wa sou demo arimasen yo.

examples.japanese: 両親の前ではいつもいい子ぶっている妹が嫌いです。
examples.english: I hate my sister who always pretends to be a good girl in front of my parents.
examples.pronunciation: ryoushin no mae de wa itsumo ii ko butte iru imouto ga kirai desu.

id: grammar:n1:べからず / べからざる
word: べからず / べからざる
meaning: must not; should not; do not ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
Fixed expression / pattern: べからず
Fixed expression / pattern: べからざる
connectionSourcePoint: べからず / べからざる

examples.japanese: ここに駐車するべからず。
examples.english: Don't park here. / No parking here.
examples.pronunciation: koko ni chuusha suru bekarazu.

examples.japanese: 芝生に入るべからず。
examples.english: Stay off the grass.
examples.pronunciation: shibafu ni hairu bekarazu.

examples.japanese: ビル内でたばこを吸うべからず。
examples.english: No smoking inside the building!
examples.pronunciation: biru nai de tabako o suu bekarazu.

examples.japanese: この池で釣りをするべからず。
examples.english: Don't fish in this pond.
examples.pronunciation: kono ike de tsuri o suru bekarazu.

examples.japanese: 彼はわがチームには欠くべからざる選手である。
examples.english: He is an indispensable player for our team.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa waga chiimu ni wa kaku bekarazaru senshu de aru.

examples.japanese: 高齢者を脅してお金を奪い取るなど許すべからざる行為だ。
examples.english: Threatening the elderly and stealing their money is an unforgivable act.
examples.pronunciation: koureisha o odoshite okane o ubaitoru nado yurusu bekarazaru koui da.

examples.japanese: 美しい音楽は映画において欠くべからざるものである。
examples.english: Beautiful music is an integral part of a movie.
examples.pronunciation: utsukushii ongaku wa eiga ni oite kaku bekarazaru mono de aru.

examples.japanese: 政治家がその地位を利用してお金を貰うは、許すべからざる犯罪だ。
examples.english: It is an unforgivable crime for politicians to use their position to earn money.
examples.pronunciation: seijika ga sono chii o riyou shite okane o morau wa, yurusu bekarazaru hanzai da.

meaning: in order to; for the purpose of ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
V（辞書形）+ べく
※「する」は「すべく」という言い方もある。
usageJapanese:
〜しようと思って / 〜ために
目的や強い意志があって〜すると言いたい時に使う。
usageNotes:
①後ろに依頼文、命令文、相手に働きかける文は来ない
②硬い表現

examples.japanese: JLPT N1に合格すべく、毎日頑張って勉強しています。
examples.english: I am studying hard every day to pass JLPT N1.
examples.pronunciation: JLPT N1 ni goukaku subeku, mainichi ganbatte benkyou shite imasu.

examples.japanese: 親の期待に応えるべく、努力して医者の道に進んだ。
examples.english: I made an effort to meet the expectations of my parents and went on to become a doctor.
examples.pronunciation: oya no kitai ni kotaeru beku, doryoku shite isha no michi ni susunda.

examples.japanese: 彼は良い席を手に入れるべく、2時間も前から並んでいた。
examples.english: He had been in line for two hours to get a good seat.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa yoi seki o te ni ireru beku, 2 jikan mo mae kara narande ita.

examples.japanese: 人手不足という問題を解決すべく、社内で緊急会議が開かれた。
examples.english: An emergency meeting was held in-house to solve the problem of labor shortage.
examples.pronunciation: hitodebusoku toiu mondai o kaiketsu subeku, shanai de kinkyuu kaigi ga akareta.

examples.japanese: 彼は一日も早く借金を返すべく、必死で働いている。
examples.english: He is working hard to pay off his debt as soon as possible.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa ichinichi mo hayaku shakkin o kaesu beku, hisshi de hataraite iru.

examples.japanese: 花見の場所を確保すべく、友達が早朝に公園へ向かった。
examples.english: A friend went to the park early in the morning to secure a place for cherry blossom viewing.
examples.pronunciation: hanami no basho o kakuho subeku, tomodachi ga souchou ni kouen e mukatta.

examples.japanese: 娘の野菜嫌いを克服すべく、色々な調理法を試しているところだ。
examples.english: I'm trying various cooking methods to overcome my daughter's dislike of vegetables.
examples.pronunciation: musume no yasaigirai o kokufuku subeku, iroiro na chourihou o tameshite iru tokoro da.

examples.japanese: 新商品を海外でも販売すべく、英語訳がついた説明書を作っています。
examples.english: We are making an instruction manual with an English translation so that we can sell new products overseas.
examples.pronunciation: shinshouhin o kaigai demo hanbai subeku, eigoyaku ga tsuita setsumeisho o tsukutte imasu.

meaning: as it is bound to (happen); following the natural course
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: べくして

examples.japanese: あの子は何も勉強せずに、遊びばかりしているんから、大学試験に落ちるべくして落ちた。
examples.english: That kid doesn’t bother studying and spends all of his time playing. Only naturally, he failed the university entrance exam.
examples.pronunciation: ano ko wa nani mo benkyou se zu ni, asobi bakari shite irun kara, daigaku shiken ni ochiru beku shite ochita.

examples.japanese: 君はいつもこんな所に自転車を置いていたから、盗むべくして盗んだ。
examples.english: If you always leave your bike in a place like this, sooner or later it will be stolen.
examples.pronunciation: kimi wa itsumo konna tokoro ni jitensha o oite ita kara, nusumu beku shite nusunda.

examples.japanese: 今回は練習不足がたたり、負けるべくして負けたのだと思う。
examples.english: I think it's only natural we lost with our lack of practice this time.
examples.pronunciation: konkai wa renshuu busoku ga tatari, makeru beku shite maketa noda to omou.

examples.japanese: 彼とは考え方よく似ていて、私たちは出会うべくして出会ったと信じている。
examples.english: Our way of thinking is very similar and I believe we met as though we were meant to meet each other.
examples.pronunciation: kare to wa kangaekata yoku nite ite, watashitachi wa deau beku shite deatta to shinjite iru.

examples.japanese: この機械の危険性は以前から何度も指摘されていた。この事故は起こるべくして起こったといえる。
examples.english: The dangers of this machine have been pointed out many times before. It can be said that this accident was bound to happen.
examples.pronunciation: kono kikai no kikensei wa izen kara nando mo shiteki sarete ita. ko no jiko wa okoru beku shite okotta to ieru.

examples.japanese: 彼が勝ったのは偶然ではない。練習につぐ練習を重ねて、彼は勝つべくして勝ったのだ。
examples.english: His victory is not by accident. Practicing day and night like that, it's only natural he would win.
examples.pronunciation: kare ga katta no wa guuzen dewa nai. renshuu ni tsugu renshuu o kasanete, kare wa katsu beku shite katta no da.

examples.japanese: 国民の不満を政府が無視し続けた結果、デモは起こるべくして起こった。
examples.english: As to be expected, demonstrations happened as a result of the government's continued ignorance of public dissatisfaction.
examples.pronunciation: kokumin no fuman o seifu ga mushi shi tsudzuketa kekka, demo wa okoru beku shite okotta.

meaning: cannot possibly be ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: べくもない

examples.japanese: 彼は勉強をせずに毎日遊んでばかりいた。N１合格は望むべくもない。
examples.english: He just played every day without studying. There is no hope of passing N1.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa benkyou o sezu ni mainichi asonde bakari ita. N1 goukaku o nozomu beku mo nai.

examples.japanese: 祖父の病状は悪くなるばかりだ。回復はもう望むべくもない。
examples.english: My grandfather's condition is only getting worse. There is no hope for recovery anymore.
examples.pronunciation: sofu no byoujou wa waruku naru bakari da. Kaifuku wa mou nozomubeku mo nai.

examples.japanese: すでに5点も差があるので、この試合に勝つのは望むべくもない。
examples.english: There is already a difference of 5 points, so I can't hope to win this match.
examples.pronunciation: sudeni 5 ten mo sa ga aru node, kono shiai ni katsu nowa nozomu beku mo nai.

examples.japanese: 東京生まれと育ちの彼には、田舎暮らしの大変さなど知るべくもない。
examples.english: Born and raised in Tokyo, he doesn't know how difficult life in the countryside is.
examples.pronunciation: toukyou umare to sodachi no kare ni wa, inakakurashi no taihensa nado shirubeku mo nai.

examples.japanese: プロとは比べるべくもないですが、彼の歌のうまさは学校の一番だと思います。
examples.english: It's hard to compare with a professional, but I think his singing is the best at school.
examples.pronunciation: puro to wa kuraberubeku mo nai desu ga, kare no uta no umasa wa gakkou no ichiban da to omoimasu.

examples.japanese: ビートルズとは比べるべくもないが、彼らは日本ではかなり有名なバンドです。
examples.english: It's hard to compare to the Beatles, but they are a pretty famous band in Japan.
examples.pronunciation: biitoruzu towa kuraberubeku mo nai ga, karera wa nihon de wa kanari yuumei na bando desu.

examples.japanese: 日本社会における少子高齢化が大きな問題であることは疑うべくもない。
examples.english: There is no doubt that the declining birthrate and aging population in Japanese society is a major problem.
examples.pronunciation: nihon shakai ni okeru shoushi koureika ga ookina mondai de aru koto wa utagaubeku mo nai.

meaning: better than; would rather ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: N / な-adj + ほうがましだ

examples.japanese: あなたと結婚するくらいなら、独身のほうがましだ。
examples.english: It's better to be single than to marry you.
examples.pronunciation: anata to kekkon suru kurai nara, dokushin no hou ga mashida.

examples.japanese: ギャンブルに金を浪費するなら捨てたほうがましだ。
examples.english: I would rather throw my money away than waste it on gambling.
examples.pronunciation: gyanburu ni kin o rouhi suru nara suteta hou ga mashida.

examples.japanese: 途中でやめるぐらいなら始めからやらないほうがましだ。
examples.english: If you're going to give up halfway, it's better to not start at all.
examples.pronunciation: tochuu de yameru gurai nara hajime kara yaranai hou ga mashida.

examples.japanese: そんな雑誌を読むくらいなら、昼寝をするほうがましだよ。
examples.english: It's better to take a nap than to read such a magazine.
examples.pronunciation: sonna zasshi o yomu kurai nara, hirune o suru hou ga mashida yo.

examples.japanese: こんな天気の中を出かけるよりは、家にいるほうがましだ。
examples.english: It's better to stay at home than to go out in this weather.
examples.pronunciation: konna tenki no naka o dekakeru yori wa, ie ni iru hou ga mashida.

examples.japanese: これを彼にあげるなら捨ててしまったほうがましだ。
examples.english: I'd rather throw it away than give it to him.
examples.pronunciation: kore o kare ni ageru nara sutete shimatta hou ga mashida.

examples.japanese: こんな苦しい思いをするくらいなら死んだほうがましだ。
examples.english: I would rather die than live and suffer like this.
examples.pronunciation: konna kurushii omoi o suru kurai nara shinda hou ga mashida.

meaning: it's not worth; no need to ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: V（辞書形）+ ほどのことではない
usageJapanese:
"大したことではないので、〜する必要はない"
程度が低く重要でないことを表す。

examples.japanese: それは悲しむほどのことではない。
examples.english: That isn't anything to be sad over.
examples.pronunciation: sore wa kanashimu hodo no koto dewa nai.

examples.japanese: もっとも気にする程のことではないが。
examples.english: You needn't let it worry you, though.
examples.pronunciation: mottomo ki ni suru hodo no koto dewa nai ga.

examples.japanese: ちょっと転んで、怪我をしただけなので、病院に行くほどのことではない。
examples.english: I just fell and got a little hurt so I don't need to go to the hospital.
examples.pronunciation: chotto koronde, kega o shita dake na node, byouin ni iku hodo no koto dewa nai.

examples.japanese: これは初級の文法だから、説明するほどのことではないですね？
examples.english: This is grammar for beginners, so there's no need to explain, right?
examples.pronunciation: kore wa shokyuu no bunpou da kara, setsumei suru hodo no koto dewa nai desu ne?

examples.japanese: この話は人に隠れてお話しするほどのことではないと思います。
examples.english: I think this story is not something that needs to be said in secret.
examples.pronunciation: kono hanashi wa hito ni kakurete ohanashi suru hodo no koto dewa nai to omoimasu.

examples.japanese: 薬を飲めば治りますから、手術するほどのことではありません。
examples.english: Taking medicine will cure the disease, so there is no need to do surgery.
examples.pronunciation: kusuri o nomeba naorimasu kara, shujutsu suru hodo no koto dewa arimasen.

examples.japanese: 今回の旅はちゃんと必要なものを準備したから、心配するほどのことではない。
examples.english: I've prepared what I need for this trip, so there's no need to worry about me.
examples.pronunciation: konkai no tabi wa chanto hitsuyou na mono o junbi shita kara, shinpai suru hodo no koto dewa nai.

examples.japanese: ちょっと熱があるだけですから、授業を休む程のことではない。
examples.english: I'm just a little feverish so there's no need to take a break from school.
examples.pronunciation: chotto netsu ga aru dake desu kara, jugyou o yasumu hodo no koto dewa nai.

meaning: to do over and over again; to do relentlessly
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: V（ます形） + まくる
usageJapanese:
とてもたくさん～する
動作をたくさん繰り返したり、継続する様子。

examples.japanese: 彼は日曜日なのに仕事をしまくっていた。
examples.english: Even though it's Sunday, he just keeps working nonstop.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa nichiyoubi na noni shigoto o shi makutte ita.

examples.japanese: 漢字を書きまくって覚える。
examples.english: To memorize kanji by just writing them repeatedly.
examples.pronunciation: kanji o kaki makutte oboeru.

examples.japanese: 日本旅行で、友達と写真を撮りまくった。
examples.english: I constantly took pictures with my friends on my trip to Japan.
examples.pronunciation: nihon ryokou de, tomodachi to shashin o tori makutta.

examples.japanese: 明日、日本語の試験なので１日勉強しまくった。
examples.english: I have a Japanese exam tomorrow so I studied hard all day long.
examples.pronunciation: ashita, nihongo no shiken nano de ichinichi benkyou shi makutta.

examples.japanese: 仕事でミスしまくって、散々上司に怒られた。
examples.english: I made a lot of mistakes at work and my boss got angry at me.
examples.pronunciation: shigoto de misu shi makutte, sanzan joushi ni okorareta.

examples.japanese: 宝くじに当たったら、お金を使いまくる。
examples.english: If I win the lottery, I’ll go on a spending spree.
examples.pronunciation: takarakuji ni atattara, okane o tsukai makuru.

examples.japanese: 一週間も雨が降りまくって、街が水没寸前になった。
examples.english: It has been raining non-stop for a week, and the streets are almost flooded.
examples.pronunciation: isshuukan mo ame ga furi makutte, machi ga suibotsu sunzen ni natta.

examples.japanese: 夏休みに入ってから、彼は毎日寝まくって、宿題は全然やってません。
examples.english: Since starting the summer vacation, he has constantly been sleeping every day and hasn't done any homework at all.
examples.pronunciation: natsuyasumi ni haitte kara, kare wa mainichi ne makutte, shukudai wa zenzen yattemasen.

meaning: should not; must not ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
V（辞書形）+ まじき
※「する」は「すまじき」と言うこともできる
usageJapanese:
〜してはいけない
職業や立場を考えると、すべきではない行為や態度について、非難の気持ちを込めて言う時に使う。
usageNotes: 「あるまじき」や「許すまじき」など、使用範囲は限られている。

examples.japanese: 生徒に暴力を振るなんて、先生としてあるまじき行為だ。
examples.english: Violence against students is something a teacher absolutely should not do
examples.pronunciation: seito ni bouryoku o furu nante, sensei to shite aru majiki koui da.

examples.japanese: 他の人の案を盗むなんて許すまじきことだ。
examples.english: Stealing someone else's ideas is an unforgivable behavior.
examples.pronunciation: hoka no hito no an o nusumu nante yurusu majiki koto da.

examples.japanese: 彼のやったことは、人としてあるまじき残酷な行為だ。
examples.english: What he did was a cruel and unacceptable action for a human being.
examples.pronunciation: kare no yatta koto wa, hito toshite aru majiki zankoku na koui da.

examples.japanese: 試験中にカンニングのような許すまじき行為をする人は０点にします。
examples.english: Those who do unforgivable acts such as cheating during the exam will be given 0 points.
examples.pronunciation: shikenchuu ni kanningu no youna yurusu majiki koii o suru hito wa 0 ten ni shimasu.

examples.japanese: 学生を恐喝するとは、教師にあるまじき行為だ。
examples.english: It is totally unacceptable for a teacher to blackmail students.
examples.pronunciation: gakusei o kyoukatsu suru to wa, kyoushi ni aru majiki koui da.

examples.japanese: 弱い者をだましてお金を取るなど、人間にあるまじき行為だ。
examples.english: Things like deceiving the weak and taking their money is not something humans should do.
examples.pronunciation: yowai mono o damashite okane o toru nado, ningen ni aru majiki koui da.

examples.japanese: 一国の首相にあるまじき発言だ。凄く無責任です。
examples.english: That is an unacceptable statement from the prime minister of a country. It is truly irresponsible.
examples.pronunciation: ikkoku no shushou ni aru majiki hatsugen da. sugoku musekinin desu.

id: grammar:n1:までだ / までのことだ
word: までだ / までのことだ
meaning: only; just; nothing else
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
N / な-adj + までだ
N / な-adj + までのことだ
connectionSourcePoint: までだ / までのことだ

examples.japanese: ちょっと聞いてみたまでだ。
examples.english: I just wanted to ask (there's nothing else to it)
examples.pronunciation: chotto kiite mita made da.

examples.japanese: この値段まで安くしてくれないなら、今回の契約をキャンセルするまでのことだ。
examples.english: If you won't lower the price, then I will just cancel the next contract.
examples.pronunciation: kono nedan made yasuku shite kurenai nara, konkai no keiyaku o kyanseru suru made no koto da.

examples.japanese: 誰も手伝ってくれないのなら、自分一人でやるまでのことです。
examples.english: If no one is willing to help you, then you can just do it by yourself.
examples.pronunciation: dare mo tetsudatte kurenai no nara, jibun hitori de yaru made no koto desu.

examples.japanese: 給料が下がるなら、会社を辞めるまでです。
examples.english: If my salary is just going to get lower, I have no choice but to quit.
examples.pronunciation: kyuuryou ga sagaru nara, kaisha o yameru made desu.

examples.japanese: 妻が反対だと言えば、諦めるまでです。
examples.english: If my wife is going to disagree, then I'll have no choice but to give up.
examples.pronunciation: tsuma ga hantai da to ieba, akirameru made desu.

examples.japanese: 風邪をひいたら、すべての予定を取りやめて寝るまでだ。
examples.english: If you catch a cold, you just have to cancel all your appointments and get some rest.
examples.pronunciation: kaze o hiitara, subete no yotei o toriyamete neru made da.

examples.japanese: いくら高い車を買っても、使わなければそれまでだ。
examples.english: It doesn't matter how expensive of a car you buy. If you don't use it, that's all there is to it.
examples.pronunciation: ikura takai kuruma o kattemo, tsukawanakereba sore made da.

examples.japanese: いくらお金を稼いでも死んでしまえばそれまでだ。
examples.english: It doesn't matter how much money you save up, once you die there's no use for it.
examples.pronunciation: ikura o kane o kaseidemo shinde shimaeba sore made da.

id: grammar:n1:までもない / までもなく
word: までもない / までもなく
meaning: there's no need to; it’s not necessary to ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
Fixed expression / pattern: までもない
Fixed expression / pattern: までもなく
connectionSourcePoint: までもない / までもなく

examples.japanese: それは問題にするまでもないことだ。
examples.english: This is not even a problem worth mentioning.
examples.pronunciation: sore wa mondai suru made mo nai koto da.

examples.japanese: アメリカが広いということは、言うまでもない。
examples.english: It goes without saying that America is a very large country.
examples.pronunciation: amerika ga hiroi to iu koto wa, iu made mo nai.

examples.japanese: アルコール検査をするまでもなく、彼が酔っ払っていることは明白だ。
examples.english: We don't need a breath alcohol test to know, he is obviously drunk.
examples.pronunciation: arukouru kensa o suru made mo naku, kare ga yopparatte iru koto wa meihaku da.

examples.japanese: その件については、部長に許可を取るまでもない。
examples.english: Regarding this topic, there's no need to get approval from the department head.
examples.pronunciation: sono ken ni tsuite wa, buchou ni kyoka o toru made mo nai.

examples.japanese: この病気は軽度ですから、手術するまでもありません。
examples.english: The disease is mild and does not require surgery.
examples.pronunciation: kono byouki wa keido desu kara, shujutsu suru made mo arimasen.

examples.japanese: これは初級の文型ですから、わざわざ説明するまでもないですね。
examples.english: This is a beginner's sentence pattern, so there is no need to explain it.
examples.pronunciation: kore wa shokyuu no bunkei desu kara, wazawaza setsumei suru made mo nai desu ne.

examples.japanese: これくらいの修理を頼むまでもない。私が直してあげるよ。
examples.english: There's no need to ask for repairs on something like this. I'll fix it for you.
examples.pronunciation: kore kurai no shuuri o tanomu made mo nai. watashi ga naoshite ageru yo.

examples.japanese: みんなには言うまでもないと思うけど、ゴミはちゃんとゴミ箱に捨ててくださいね。
examples.english: This should be needless to say to everyone, but please throw your trash in the trash can.
examples.pronunciation: minna ni wa iu made mo nai to omou kedo, gomi wa chanto gomibako ni sutete kudasai ne.

meaning: covered with; stained; smeared with ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: N + まみれ
usageJapanese:
〜がたくさん付いている
表面に汚いものがたくさん付いていて、汚れている様子を表す。
usageNotes:
[よく一緒に使う言葉]
血、泥、ほこり、砂、汗、油

examples.japanese: 彼は汗まみれだった。
examples.english: He was drenched in sweat.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa ase mamire datta.

examples.japanese: 息子が泥まみれになって帰って来た。
examples.english: My son came home covered in mud.
examples.pronunciation: musuko ga doro mamire ni natte kaette kita.

examples.japanese: 彼の袖が油まみれのなべに触れた。
examples.english: His sleeve touched the greasy pan.
examples.pronunciation: kare no sode ga abura mamire no nabe ni fureta.

examples.japanese: ビーチへ行ったとき、砂まみれになって遊んだ。
examples.english: When I went to the beach, I got covered in sand while playing around.
examples.pronunciation: biichi e itta toki, suna mamire ni natte asonda.

examples.japanese: 彼女は汗まみれになって、一生懸命バレーボールの練習をしている。
examples.english: She's completely drenched in sweat, giving her all in volleyball practice.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa ase mamire ninatte, isshoukenmei bareebooru no renshuu o shiteiru.

examples.japanese: 誰か救急車を呼んでください！この人が血まみれになって倒れている。
examples.english: Somebody call and ambulance! This person is collapsed and covered in blood.
examples.pronunciation: dareka kyuukyuusha o yonde kudasai! kono hito ga chi mamire ni natte taoreteiru.

examples.japanese: 彼は借金まみれになったあげく破産してしまいました。
examples.english: As a result of being piled up in debt, he filed for bankruptcy.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa shakkin mamire ni natta ageku hasan shite shimaimashita.

meaning: completely; absolutely; totally; (not) at all​ ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: まるっきり

examples.japanese: これは私の欲しいものとまるっきり反対だ。
examples.english: This is exactly the opposite of what I want.
examples.pronunciation: kore wa watashi no hoshii mono to marukkiri hantai da.

examples.japanese: 彼はオペラについてまるっきり知らない。
examples.english: He doesn't know anything about opera.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa opera ni tsuite marukkiri shiranai.

examples.japanese: 翻訳の仕事はまるっきり初めてなので、いろいろ教えてください。
examples.english: This is my very first time working as a translator, so please teach me various things.
examples.pronunciation: honyaku no shigoto wa marukkiri hajimete na node, iroiro oshiete kudasai.

examples.japanese: 私は英語がまるっきり話せませんが、秘書は大丈夫です。
examples.english: I can't speak English at all, but my secretary is fine.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa eigo ga marukkiri hanasemasen ga, hisho wa daijoubu desu.

examples.japanese: あなたは私の言うことなんかまるっきり信じてないんでしょう?
examples.english: You don't really believe what I say, right?
examples.pronunciation: anata wa watashi no iu koto nanka marukkiri shinji tenain deshou?

examples.japanese: JLPTの試験が終わると、日本語の勉強する気がまるっきりなくなってしまった。
examples.english: After the JLPT exam, I completely lost the desire to study Japanese.
examples.pronunciation: JLPT no shiken ga owaru to, nihongo no benkyou suru ki ga marukkiri nakunatte shimatta.

examples.japanese: 会議で意見を出したのに、まるっきり無視されてしまった。
examples.english: I gave my opinion at the meeting, but it was completely ignored.
examples.pronunciation: kaigi de iken o dashita no ni, marukkiri mushi sarete shimatta.

meaning: seems; show signs of; to have the appearance of ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: N + めく
usageJapanese: 完全に〜とは言えないが、〜ように感じられる
usageNotes: 春、冗談、皮肉、謎、説教、秘密などの接続される単語は限定される。

examples.japanese: 暑くなって、ずいぶん夏めいてきましたね。
examples.english: The weather is getting hotter, it seems that summer is here.
examples.pronunciation: atsuku natte, zuibun natsu meite kimashita ne.

examples.japanese: あの人はもう二十歳なのに、子供めいた行動がよくします。
examples.english: Even though he is 20 years old, he often behaves like a child.
examples.pronunciation: ano hito wa mou hatachi na noni, kodomo meita koudou ga yoku shimasu.

examples.japanese: あの子はまだ中学校の一年生なのに、よく大人めいた服を着ます。
examples.english: Even though she is still in the first year of junior high school, she often wears mature clothes.
examples.pronunciation: ano ko wa mada chuugakkou no ichinensei na noni, yoku otona meita fuku o kimasu.

examples.japanese: 彼はどこか謎めいた雰囲気があり、女性に人気がある。
examples.english: He has a mysterious atmosphere and is popular with women.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa doko ka nazo meita funiki ga ari, josei ni ninki ga aru.

examples.japanese: 先生の皮肉めいた言い方を、生徒は不快に感じている 。
examples.english: It seems that the students feel uncomfortable because of the teacher's sarcastic way of speaking.
examples.pronunciation: sensei no hiniku meita iikata o, seito wa fukai ni kanjite iru.

examples.japanese: 別れた彼氏から、脅迫めいたメールが届いて、怖くなった。
examples.english: I got scared when I received threatening emails from my ex-boyfriend.
examples.pronunciation: wakareta kareshi kara, kyouhaku meita meeru ga todoite, kowakunatta.

examples.japanese: その記事に対する反論めいたメールが大量に出版社に届いた。
examples.english: A large number of e-mails appearing to object against that article have arrived at the publisher.
examples.pronunciation: sono kiji ni taisuru hanron meita meeru ga tairyou ni shuppansha ni todoita.

meaning: one may well say so, but~; not only... but also ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: N + もさることながら
usageJapanese: 〜は当然そうだが、その他にも・・・だ
usageNotes: 文の後の内容が強調される。

examples.japanese: 彼女は外見もさることながら性格もとてもいいです。
examples.english: Of course she looks very nice, but her personality is also very good.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa gaiken mo saru koto nagara seikaku mo totemo ii desu.

examples.japanese: 試合に勝つには選手の実力もさることながら、運も必要なのだ。
examples.english: Winning the match is not only based on the ability of the players, but luck is also essential.
examples.pronunciation: shiai ni katsu ni wa senshu no jitsuryoku mo saru koto nagara, un mo hitsuyou na no da.

examples.japanese: このカメラは、価格もさることながら、デザインもよいので人気がある。
examples.english: This camera is popular because of it's price, but it's design is also very good.
examples.pronunciation: kono kamera wa, kakaku mo saru koto nagara, dezain mo yoi node ninki ga aru.

examples.japanese: 最近は忙しすぎて、疲労もさることながら、ストレスもピークに達している。
examples.english: Recently, I've been so busy that I'm not only tired but also very stressed.
examples.pronunciation: saikin wa isogashi sugite, hirou mo saru koto nagara, sutoresu mo piiku ni tasshite iru.

examples.japanese: この映画は話の内容もさることながら映像の美しさも評判がいい。
examples.english: This movie has a good reputation not only for the content of the story but also for the beauty of the footage.
examples.pronunciation: kono eiga wa hanashi no naiyou mo saru koto nagara eizou no utsukushisa mo hyouban ga ii.

examples.japanese: あの男はビジネスの知識もさることながら、プレゼンのスキルも非常に高い。
examples.english: Not only does that man have general business knowledge, but his presentation skill is extremely high.
examples.pronunciation: ano otoko wa bijinesu no chishiki mo saru koto nagara, purezen no sukiru mo hijou ni takai.

examples.japanese: 彼のプレゼンは内容もさることながら、話し方がわかりやすくとても素晴らしいものだった。
examples.english: Not only was his presentation content good, but his way of speakin was very easy to understand and overall it was a great performance.
examples.pronunciation: kare no purezen wa naiyou mo saru koto nagara, hanashikata ga wakari yasuku totemo subarashii mono datta.

meaning: or; otherwise
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: sentence / phrase + もしくは + sentence / phrase

examples.japanese: 遅刻するの場合は先生もしくは僕に連絡してください。
examples.english: If you are late, please contact your teacher or me.
examples.pronunciation: chikoku suru no baai wa sensei moshikuwa boku ni renraku shite kudasai.

examples.japanese: お問い合わせは、電話もしくはファックスでお願いします。
examples.english: For inquiries, please contact us by phone or fax.
examples.pronunciation: otoiawase wa, denwa moshikuwa fakkusu de onegaishimasu.

examples.japanese: あの男は弁護士もしくは裁判官ですか。
examples.english: Is that man a lawyer or a judge?
examples.pronunciation: ano otoko wa bengoshi moshikuwa saibankan desu ka.

examples.japanese: 参加費はクレジットカードもしくは現金で払うことができます。
examples.english: The participation fee can be paid by credit card or by cash.
examples.pronunciation: sankahi wa kurejitto kaado moshikuwa genkin de harau koto ga dekimasu.

examples.japanese: この施設は、会員もしくはその家族に限り使用できる。
examples.english: This facility can only be used by members or their families.
examples.pronunciation: kono shisetsu wa, kaiin moshikuwa sono kazoku ni kagiri shiyou dekiru.

examples.japanese: 明日は社長もしくは部長が新計画を発表なさります。
examples.english: Tomorrow, the president or general manager will announce a new plan.
examples.pronunciation: ashita wa shachou moshikuwa buchou ga shinkeikaku o happyou nasarimasu.

examples.japanese: 同封の許可証にご両親もしくは保護者の同意署名をもらってください。
examples.english: Please obtain the consent signature of your parents or guardians on the enclosed permit.
examples.pronunciation: doufuu no kyokashou ni goryoushin moshikuwa hogosha no doui shomei o moratte kudasai.

meaning: without even doing ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: もしないで

examples.japanese: 彼女は、いつもやりもしないで、できないと言う。
examples.english: She always gives up before she even tries.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa itsumo yari mo shinai de, dekinai to iu.

examples.japanese: 食べもしないで、まずいかどうかなんてわからないでしょ。
examples.english: You can't know if it's bad or not without eating it first, right?
examples.pronunciation: tabe mo shinai de, mazui ka dou ka nante wakaranai desho.

examples.japanese: 食事もしないでゲームばかりしていると、病気になるよ。
examples.english: If you just play games without eating anything, you will get sick.
examples.pronunciation: shokuji mo shinai de geemu bakari shite iru to, byouki ni naru yo.

examples.japanese: 知りもしないで偉そうなことを言うな。
examples.english: Don't act like you're an expert when you don't know much about it.
examples.pronunciation: shiri mo shinai de erasou na koto o iu na.

examples.japanese: 彼は授業に遅れてきたのに謝りもしないで席に着いた。
examples.english: Although he was late for class, he took his seat without apologizing.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa jugyou ni okurete kita noni ayamari mo shinai de seki ni tsuita.

examples.japanese: 気持ちを相手に伝えもしないで、自分のことを解ってほしいって無理でしょ。
examples.english: You can't expect people to understand you without telling them how you feel.
examples.pronunciation: kimochi o aite ni tsutae mo shinai de, jibun no koto o wakatte hoshiitte muri desho.

examples.japanese: その女優さんは彼女に声をかけたファンに見向きもしないで行ってしまった。
examples.english: The actress left without even looking at the fans who talked to her.
examples.pronunciation: sono joyuu san wa kanojo ni koe o kaketa fan ni mimuki mo shinai de itte shimatta.

meaning: because; for that reason
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: もので

examples.japanese: 朝寝坊したもので、授業に遅刻してしまいました。
examples.english: I overslept in the morning and was late for class.
examples.pronunciation: asanebou shita mono de, jugyou ni chikoku shite shimai mashita.

examples.japanese: 日本語ができないもので、日本での生活は大変でした。
examples.english: I couldn't speak Japanese, so life in Japan was difficult.
examples.pronunciation: nihongo ga dekinai mono de, nihon de no seikatsu wa taihen deshita.

examples.japanese: 子どもが病気になったもので、一週間の休みを取った。
examples.english: My child got sick and took a week off.
examples.pronunciation: kodomo ga byouki ni natta mono de, isshuukan no yasumi o totta.

examples.japanese: ごめんなさい！出張したもので、すぐにあなたのメールを返事できませんでした。
examples.english: I'm sorry! I was on a business trip and couldn't reply to your email right away.
examples.pronunciation: gomennasai! shucchou shita mono de, sugu ni anata no meeru o henji dekimasen deshita.

examples.japanese: 最近、日本語を使わなかったもので、レベルが落ちた。
examples.english: Recently, I haven't used Japanese, so my level dropped.
examples.pronunciation: saikin, nihongo o tsukawanakatta mono de, reberu ga ochita.

examples.japanese: 車の事故があったもので一か月学校を休んだ。
examples.english: I was in a car accident and absent from school for a month.
examples.pronunciation: kuruma no jiko ga atta mono de ikkagetsu gakkou o yasunda.

examples.japanese: 妻が妊娠したもので僕はたばこを辞めることにした。
examples.english: My wife became pregnant and so I decided to quit smoking.
examples.pronunciation: tsuma ga ninshin shita mono de boku wa tabako o yameru koto ni shita.

meaning: to assume; to suppose; on the presumption that ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: ものとして

examples.japanese: スミスさんはもう来ないものとして、始めましょう。
examples.english: Let's assume Mr. Smith is not coming and get started.
examples.pronunciation: sumisu san wa mou konai mono toshite, hajimemashou.

examples.japanese: 何も知らないものとして説明してください。
examples.english: Please explain it to me as though I know nothing.
examples.pronunciation: nanimo shiranai mono toshite setsumei shite kudasai.

examples.japanese: 予算はそれしかないものとして準備してくれ。
examples.english: Please make preparations under the assumption that this is all the budget we have.
examples.pronunciation: yosan wa sore shika nai mono toshite junbi shite kure.

examples.japanese: 私はいないものとして、授業を続いてください。
examples.english: Please continue the lesson as though I were not here.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa inai mono toshite, jugyou o tsuzuite kudasai.

examples.japanese: 初級漢字は出来るものとして、中級漢字のクラスを取った。
examples.english: I took the mid-level kanji class assuming I understood basic kanji.
examples.pronunciation: shokyuu kanji wa dekiru mono toshite, chuukyuu kanji no kurasu o totta.

examples.japanese: たぶん行けないと思うので、私はいないものとして、話を進めてください。
examples.english: I don't think I will be able to go, so please continue the talk assuming I will not be there.
examples.pronunciation: tabun ikenai to omou no de, watashi wa inai mono toshite, hanashi o susumete kudasai.

meaning: shall; to assume; understood as ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: ものとする

examples.japanese: 第５条議長は全会議を主宰するものとする。
examples.english: Article 5. The president shall preside at all meetings.
examples.pronunciation: dai 5 jou gichou wa zenkaigi o shusai suru mono to suru.

examples.japanese: 科料は2万円を超えないものとする。
examples.english: The fine shall not exceed 20,000 yen.
examples.pronunciation: karyou wa 2 man en o koenai mono to suru.

examples.japanese: 契約の延長は、双方の同意によるものとする。
examples.english: The contract extension must be agreed by both parties.
examples.pronunciation: keiyaku no enchou wa, souhou no doui ni yoru mono to suru.

examples.japanese: 住所変更の通知は、その到達によって効力が発生するものとする 。
examples.english: Notice of a change of address shall be effective only upon receipt of concerned parties.
examples.pronunciation: jusho henkou no tsuuchi wa, sono toutatsu ni yotte kouryoku ga hassei suru mono to suru.

examples.japanese: 委員会の決定は，その委員の過半数によるものとする。
examples.english: The board shall take its decisions by majority.
examples.pronunciation: iinkai no kettei wa, sono iin no kahansuu ni yoru mono to suru.

examples.japanese: 商標は，登録した後に公告するものとする。
examples.english: The trade mark shall be published after registration.
examples.pronunciation: shouhyou wa, touroku shita go ni koukoku suru mono to suru.

examples.japanese: 本契約書は日本の法律に準拠し、日本の法律に従って解釈され、強制されるものとする。
examples.english: This agreement shall be governed by, and construed and enforced in accordance with the law of Japan.
examples.pronunciation: honkeiyakusho wa nihon no houritsu ni junkyo shi, nihon no houritsu ni shitagatte kaishaku sare, kyousei sareru mono to suru.

id: grammar:n1:ものと思われる / ものと見られる（ものとおもわれる / ものとみられる）
word: ものと思われる / ものと見られる（ものとおもわれる / ものとみられる）
meaning: to think; to suppose; it is believed/expected that ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
Fixed expression / pattern: ものと思われる
Fixed expression / pattern: ものと見られる（ものとおもわれる
Fixed expression / pattern: ものとみられる）
connectionSourcePoint: ものと思われる / ものと見られる（ものとおもわれる / ものとみられる）

examples.japanese: この店のパスタは冷凍したものと思われる。
examples.english: The pasta in this shop is supposedly frozen.
examples.pronunciation: kono mise no pasuta wa reitou shita mono to omowareru.

examples.japanese: 日本の少子化問題はさらに進むと見られている。
examples.english: The problem of Japan's declining birthrate is expected to go further.
examples.pronunciation: nihon no shoushika mondai wa sarani susumu to mirarete iru.

examples.japanese: 彼は、今一番人気のあるアイドルだと見られている。
examples.english: He is believed to be the most popular idol right now.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa, ima ichiban ninki no aru aidoru da to mirarete iru.

examples.japanese: 兄の普段の成績から、合格は間違いないと見られている。
examples.english: From his brother's usual grades, it is believed that he will pass.
examples.pronunciation: ani no fudan no seiseki kara, gougaku wa machigainai to mirareteiru.

examples.japanese: 愛知県では、今後も大きいな地震が来ると思われている。
examples.english: It is expected that a large earthquake will occur in Aichi Prefecture.
examples.pronunciation: aichi ken de wa, kongo mo ookiina jishin ga kuru to omowareteiru.

examples.japanese: あの事件は事故ではなく、殺人である可能性が高いと見られている。
examples.english: It is believed that the incident was likely a murder, not an accident.
examples.pronunciation: ano jiken wa jiko dewa naku, satsujin de aru kanousei ga takai to mirarete iru.

examples.japanese: 近くの茂みから犯人が使ったものと思われる凶器が発見された。
examples.english: A weapon believed to have been used by the criminal was found in a nearby bush.
examples.pronunciation: chikaku no shigemi kara han'nin ga tsukatta mono to omowareru kyouki ga hakken sareta.

meaning: if only; I wish; I'm sorry it's not actually (regret)
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
V（普通形）+ ものを
イA（普通形）+ ものを
ナA（普通形）+ ものを （ナAな + ものを）
usageJapanese: 〜すれば、そうならかなったのに
usageNotes:
①話し手の不満や後悔などの気持ちが含まれる。
②「ものを」の後ろが省略される場合もある。

examples.japanese: お金が無いなら、言ってくれたらよかったものを。
examples.english: I wish you had told me you didn't have any money.
examples.pronunciation: okane ga nai nara, itte kuretara yokatta mono o.

examples.japanese: ちょっと知らせてくれたらいいものを。
examples.english: I wish you had notified me.
examples.pronunciation: chotto shirasete kuretara ii mono o.

examples.japanese: 黙っていればいいものを、つい余計なことを言ってしまった。
examples.english: I ended up saying something unnecessary and regret not keeping my mouth shut.
examples.pronunciation: damatte ireba ii mono o, tsui yokei na koto o itte shimatta.

examples.japanese: もう少し頑張れば合格するものを、惜しかったね。
examples.english: If only you worked a little harder, you would've passed. You were really close.
examples.pronunciation: mou sukoshi ganbareba goukaku suru mono o, oshikattane.

examples.japanese: やればできるものを、なんでやらないの？
examples.english: If only you tried you could do it, so why don't you?
examples.pronunciation: yareba dekiru mono o, nande yaranai no?

examples.japanese: 黙っていればバレなかったものを、どうして言っちゃうかなあ。
examples.english: If only they had kept their mouth shut, no one would have found out. I wonder why they talked about it?
examples.pronunciation: damatte ireba barenakatta mono o, doushite icchau kanaa?

examples.japanese: もっと早く医者に行けばよかったものを（どうして今まで放っておいたんですか）。
examples.english: If only you had gone to the doctor sooner... (why didn't you go all this time?)
examples.pronunciation: motto hayaku isha ni ikeba yokatta mono o (doushite ima made houtte oitan desu ka?)

meaning: already; now; no longer; not anymore
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: もはや

examples.japanese: もはや意味はありません。
examples.english: It's meaningless now.
examples.pronunciation: mohaya imi wa arimasen.

examples.japanese: 彼はもはやここでは働いていません。
examples.english: He is no longer working here.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa mohaya koko dewa hataraite imasen.

examples.japanese: 彼らにはもはや時間がない。
examples.english: They no longer have time.
examples.pronunciation: karera ni wa mohaya jkan ga nai.

examples.japanese: その規則はもはや効力がない。
examples.english: That rule is no longer in effect.
examples.pronunciation: sono kisoku wa mohaya kouryoku ga nai.

examples.japanese: もはや彼の言うことを信じない。
examples.english: I don't believe him anymore.
examples.pronunciation: mohaya kare no iu koto o shinjinai.

examples.japanese: もはやそれに関して私にできることは何もない。
examples.english: There is nothing I can do about it anymore.
examples.pronunciation: mohaya sore ni kanshite watashi ni dekiru koto wa nani mo nai.

examples.japanese: 私はもはや彼が私を見守っていると感じない。
examples.english: I no longer feel that he is watching over me.
examples.pronunciation: watashi wa mohaya kare ga watashi o mimamotte iru to kanjinai.

examples.japanese: その犬は疲れてもはや動くことができませんでした。
examples.english: That dog was tired and couldn't move anymore.
examples.pronunciation: sono inu wa tsukarete mohaya ugoku koto ga dekimasen deshita.

id: grammar:n1:も同然だ（もどうぜんだ）
word: も同然だ（もどうぜんだ）
meaning: just like; same as~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
V（タ形 / ナイ形）+ も同然
N + (も)同然だ
usageJapanese:
〜とほとんど同じだ
実際とは異なるが、ほとんど同じ状態である、様子であると言いたい時に使う。

examples.japanese: 友達ですが、子供の頃からずっとそばにいるので兄弟も同然です。
examples.english: He is my friend, but we have always been together since our childhood so he is just like my brother.
examples.pronunciation: tomodachi desu ga, kodomo no koro kara zutto soba ni iru node kyoudai mo douzen desu.

examples.japanese: この車は中古車といっても新品も同然だ。
examples.english: Although this is a used car, it is almost as good as a new one.
examples.pronunciation: kono kuruma wa chuukosha to itte mo shinpin mo douzen da.

examples.japanese: 目標がないなら死んだも同然だ。
examples.english: Having no goals is just like being dead.
examples.pronunciation: mokuhyou ga nai nara shinda mo douzen da.

examples.japanese: 社会人になったけど、毎日試験の勉強をしているので、学生同然の生活だ。
examples.english: I became a working adult, but every day I am studying for exams so my life is just like a student's.
examples.pronunciation: shakaijin ni natta kedo, mainichi shiken benkyou o shite iru no de, gakusei douzen no seikatsu da.

examples.japanese: 彼女は料理が上手で、プロ同然といっても過言ではない。
examples.english: She's good at cooking, and it's no exaggeration to say she's at a professional level.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa ryouri ga jouzu de, puro douzen to itte mo kagon de wa nai.

examples.japanese: 彼女と2年間一緒に住んでいたから、これは結婚生活も同然だ。
examples.english: I lived together for 2 years, so it's just like married life.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo to 2 nenkan isshoni sundeita kara, kore wa kekkon seikatsu mo douzen da.

examples.japanese: 1点の差だけがあったが、これでも負けるも同然だ。
examples.english: There may have just been a difference of 1 point, but even so this is the same thing as losing.
examples.pronunciation: ichi ten no sa dake ga atta ga, kore demo makeru mo douzen da.

examples.japanese: 彼の給料は家賃と生活費で使ったので、財産はないも同然だ。
examples.english: Since his salary is spent on rent and living expenses, it's the same as having no assets.
examples.pronunciation: kare no kyuuryou wa yachin to seikatsuhi de tsukatta no de, zaisan wa nai mo douzen da.

meaning: should do, but don't; absolutely not; there's no way ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: やしない

examples.japanese: 最近、息子は私の言うことなど、聞きやしない。
examples.english: Recently, my son doesn't listen to anything I say.
examples.pronunciation: saikin, musuko wa watashi no iu koto nado, kikiyashinai.

examples.japanese: うちの会社は日曜日なのに休みやしない。
examples.english: Our company doesn't take a day off even though it's Sunday.
examples.pronunciation: uchi no kaisha wa nichiyoubi na no ni yasumiyashinai.

examples.japanese: 大学時代に恋愛に夢中で勉強やしなかった。それでも卒業出来て良かったと今も思うよ。
examples.english: When I was in college, I was absorbed in love and didn't study. I'm glad I still was able to graduate.
examples.pronunciation: daigaku jidai ni renai ni muchuu de benkyou yashinakatta. soredemo sotsugyou dekite yokatta to ima mo omou yo.

examples.japanese: あの研修生は全くの役立たずだ。コーヒーの作りも知りやしない。
examples.english: That trainee is completely useless. They don't even know how to make coffee.
examples.pronunciation: ano kenshuusei wa mattaku no yakutatazu da. koohii no tsukuri mo shiriyashinai.

examples.japanese: 僕は病気があるからこんな強い酒を飲めやしない。
examples.english: I have a sickness, so I can't drink such a strong drink.
examples.pronunciation: boku wa byouki ga aru kara konna tsuyoi sake o nomeyashinai.

examples.japanese: 俺は結婚しているからあんなところに行きやしない。
examples.english: I'm married so I definitely won't go to that kind of place.
examples.pronunciation: ore wa kekkon shite iru kara anna tokoro ni ikiyashinai.

examples.japanese: 助けを求めて叫んでも無駄だぞ。誰にも聞こえやしない。
examples.english: It's no use screaming for help. There's no way anyone will hear you.
examples.pronunciation: tasuke o motomete sakende mo muda da zo. dare nimo kikoeyashinai.

meaning: pattern to give two representative examples (usually negative)
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: やれ + ... + やれ

examples.japanese: やれ出張だの、やれ会議だのと、ゆっくりする暇もない。
examples.english: With business trips and meetings, I don't have any time to relax.
examples.pronunciation: yare shucchou da no, yare kaigi da no to, yukkuri suru hima mo nai.

examples.japanese: 日本で部屋を借りる場合、やれ敷金だやれ礼金だと、いろいろなお金がかかる。
examples.english: When renting a room in Japan, it costs a lot of money from the security deposit to key money and other various fees.
examples.pronunciation: nihon de heya o kariru baai, yare shikikin da yare reikin da to, iroirona okane ga kakaru.

examples.japanese: 日本でのやれ地震だ、やれ台風だと心配事が多い。
examples.english: There are many worries like typhoons and earthquakes in Japan.
examples.pronunciation: nihon de no yare jishin da, yare taifuu da to shinpai koto ga ooi.

examples.japanese: 彼女は最近やれ剣道だ、やれ茶道だとお忙しそうだ。
examples.english: She seems to be busy with Kendo and Tea Ceremony club lately.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo wa saikin yare kendou da, yare sadou da to o isogashi sou da.

examples.japanese: 妻はやれ仕事だのやれ家事だのとやったから、自分の時間があまりない。
examples.english: Between working and doing housework, my wife doesn't have much time for herself.
examples.pronunciation: tsuma wa yare shigoto dano yare kaji dano to yatta kara, jibun no jikan ga amari nai.

examples.japanese: 息子はやれハンバーガーだやれピザだと、健康に良くないものばかりを食べたい。
examples.english: My son only wants to eat foods that are bad for his health, like hamburgers and pizzas.
examples.pronunciation: musuko wa yare hanbaagaa da yare piza da to, kenkou ni yokunai mono bakari o tabetai.

examples.japanese: 当初はやれ絵が下手だ、やれ展開が不自然だと馬鹿にされていたが、今では大人気漫画になっている。
examples.english: At first, it was ridiculed that the drawing was bad and that the development was unnatural, but now it has become a very popular manga.
examples.pronunciation: tousho wa yare e ga heta da, yare tenkai ga fushizen da to baka ni sarete ita ga, ima dewa daininki manga ni natte iru.

meaning: as soon as; the moment ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: Fixed expression / pattern: や否や

examples.japanese: 先生が教室に入るや否や、学生たちはおしゃべりを辞めた。
examples.english: As soon as the teacher entered the classroom, the students stopped talking.
examples.pronunciation: sensei ga kyoushitsu ni hairu ya ina ya, gakusei tachi wa oshaberi o yameta.

examples.japanese: 友達の赤ん坊が私の顔を見るや否や、泣き出した。
examples.english: As soon as my friend's baby saw my face, it started crying.
examples.pronunciation: tomodachi no akanbou ga watashi no kao o miru ya ina ya, naki dashita.

examples.japanese: 彼女が現れるや否や、大勢のファンが取り巻いた。
examples.english: As soon as she appeared, many fans surrounded her.
examples.pronunciation: kanojo ga arawareru ya ina ya, oozei no fan ga torimaita.

examples.japanese: 社長は部屋に入るや否や、静かになりました。
examples.english: As soon as the boss entered the room, it suddenly got quiet.
examples.pronunciation: shachou wa heya ni hairu ya ina ya, shizuka ni narimashita.

examples.japanese: 彼は電車を降りるや否や走り出したがどうやら乗り換えの電車に間に合わなかったようだ。
examples.english: He ran as soon as he got off the train for his transfer, but it doesn't look like he made his transfer in time..
examples.pronunciation: kare wa densha o oriru ya ina ya hashiri dashita ga douyara norikae no densha ni maniawana katta you da.

examples.japanese: あの子どもは、それを口に入れるや、吐き出した。
examples.english: That child vomited the instant they put that in their mouth.
examples.pronunciation: ano kodomo wa, sore o kuchi ni ireru ya, hakidashita.

examples.japanese: ドアが開くや、ワンちゃんが飛び出した！
examples.english: The moment the door opened, a dog dashed out!
examples.pronunciation: doa ga hiraku ya, wanchan ga tobidashita.

examples.japanese: 火災発生のベルが鳴るや、消防士たちは部屋を飛び出した。
examples.english: As soon as the fire bell rang, the firefighters jumped out of the room.
examples.pronunciation: kasai hassei no beru ga naru ya, shouboushi tachi wa heya o tobidashita.

meaning: therefore; consequently​; because of; due to ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: 普通形 + ゆえに
usageJapanese:
〜のために
硬い表現で文語的である。接続詞として文頭に使うこともできる。

examples.japanese: 貧しさゆえに、彼は盗みを動いた。
examples.english: As a consequence of his poverty, he ended up stealing.
examples.pronunciation: mazushisa yue ni, kare wa nusumi o ugoita.

examples.japanese: 彼は性格がおかしいゆえに友達ができなかった。
examples.english: He couldn't make friends because of his strange personality.
examples.pronunciation: kare wa seikaku ga okashii yue ni tomodachi ga dekinakatta.

examples.japanese: 悪天候ゆえに旅行は延期された。
examples.english: The trip was postponed due to bad weather.
examples.pronunciation: akutenkou yueni ryokou wa enki sareta.

examples.japanese: 日本語を知らなかったがゆえに誤解された。
examples.english: I was misunderstood because I didn't know Japanese.
examples.pronunciation: nihongo o shiranakatta ga yue ni gokai sareta.

examples.japanese: 女性であるがゆえに差別されることがある。
examples.english: You may be discriminated against because you are a woman.
examples.pronunciation: josei de aru ga yue ni sabetsu sareru koto ga aru.

examples.japanese: 彼が交通規則を守らなかったがゆえに、事故が起きてしまった。
examples.english: The accident happened because he didn't follow the traffic rules.
examples.pronunciation: kare ga koutsuu kisoku o mamoranakatta ga yue ni, jiko ga okite shimatta.

examples.japanese: 日本は経済大国であるゆえに、外国から働きに来る人も多い。
examples.english: Since Japan is an economic powerhouse, many people come to work there from abroad.
examples.pronunciation: nihon wa keizai taikoku de aru yue ni, gaikoku kara hataraki ni kuru hito mo ooi.

examples.japanese: 政府の対応が遅れたゆえに、地震の被害が大きくなった。
examples.english: Due to the delay in the government's response, the damage caused by the earthquake increased.
examples.pronunciation: seifu no taiou ga okureta yue ni, jishin no higai ga ookiku natta.

meaning: even if; no matter how/what ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
V（意向形） + ようが
V（意向形） + ようと

examples.japanese: 君が何歳であろうが関係ない。仕事が出来ればいい。
examples.english: As long as you can do the job, it doesn't matter how old you are.
examples.pronunciation: kimi ga nansai de arou ga kankei nai. shigoto ga dekireba ii.

examples.japanese: 誰と結婚しようが、私の勝手でしょ。
examples.english: Who I marry is my choice.
examples.pronunciation: dare to kekkon shiyou ga, watashi no katte desho.

examples.japanese: あなたがどんなに謝ろうとも、今回は許しません。
examples.english: Now matter how much you apologize, I'm not going to forgive you this time.
examples.pronunciation: anata ga donnani ayamarou tomo, konkai wa yurushimasen.

examples.japanese: このマンションはどんなに騒ごうとも、外からは何も聞こえません。
examples.english: No matter how loud you are in this apartment, you can't hear anything from outside.
examples.pronunciation: kono manshon wa donnani sawagou tomo, soto kara wa nanimo kikoemasen.

examples.japanese: 必要だから、どんなに高かろうがこれを買わないといけません。
examples.english: It's necessary, so I will buy it no matter how high the price may be.
examples.pronunciation: hitsuyou dakara, donnani takakarou ga kore wo kawanaito ikemasen.

examples.japanese: 僕が何をしようが勝手だ。あなたに言う必要はない。
examples.english: Whatever I do is my choice and none of your business.
examples.pronunciation: boku ga nani wo shiyou ga katte da. anata ni iu hitsuyou wa nai.

examples.japanese: 電車が遅れたせいで、どんなに急ごうと、飛行機の時間に間に合いそうにない。
examples.english: Because the train is running late, no matter how much I hurry it doesn't look like I will make the flight on time.
examples.pronunciation: densha ga okureta sei de, donna ni isogou to, hikouki no jikan ni ma ni ai sou ni nai.

id: grammar:n1:ようが～ようが / ようと～ようと
word: ようが～ようが / ようと～ようと
meaning: no matter; whether; even if [A] or [B]
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
ようが + ... + ようが
ようと + ... + ようと
connectionSourcePoint: ようが～ようが / ようと～ようと

examples.japanese: 俺は魚だろうが肉だろうが、何でも食べる。
examples.english: Whether it be fish or meat, I'll eat anything.
examples.pronunciation: ore wa sakana darou ga niku darou ga, nandemo taberu.

examples.japanese: 雨が降ろうと雪が降ろうと明日のイベントには必ず行くよ。
examples.english: Come rain or snow, I'm definitely going to tomorrow's event!
examples.pronunciation: ame ga furou to yuki ga furou to ashita no ibento ni wa kanarazu iku yo.

examples.japanese: 速かろうがゆっくりであろうが、注意して運転しなさい。
examples.english: Whether you drive fast or slow, drive carefully.
examples.pronunciation: hayakarou ga yukkuri de arou ga, chuui shite unten shinasai.

examples.japanese: 良かろうが悪かろうが、自分の子だ。
examples.english: Whether good or bad, it's my own child.
examples.pronunciation: yokarou ga warukarou ga, jibun no ko da.

examples.japanese: 新品であろうと、中古であろうと、そんな型の古いパソコンは買うべきではないと思います。
examples.english: Whether that is a new or used, I don't think you should buy such and old model..
examples.pronunciation: shinpin de arou to, chuuko de arou to, sonna kata no furui pasokon wa kau beki de wa nai to omoimasu.

examples.japanese: 毎日、時間があろうとなかろうと、連絡だけは入れてください。
examples.english: Please make sure to contact me every day, whether you are busy or not.
examples.pronunciation: mainichi, jikan ga arou to nakarou to, renraku dake wa irete kudasai.

examples.japanese: 君が病気になろうが、貧乏であろうが、僕は君を一生愛す。
examples.english: No matter if you are sick or poor, I will love you forever.
examples.pronunciation: kimi ga byouki ni narou ga, binbou de arou ga, boku wa kimi wo isshou aisu.

id: grammar:n1:ようと～まいと / ようが～まいが
word: ようと～まいと / ようが～まいが
meaning: whether or not; regardless
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
ようと + ... + まいと
ようが + ... + まいが
connectionSourcePoint: ようと～まいと / ようが～まいが

examples.japanese: 彼が来ようが来まいが、私とは関係ない。
examples.english: It doesn't matter to me whether he comes or not.
examples.pronunciation: kare ga koyou ga komai ga, watashi to wa kankei nai.

examples.japanese: 雨が降ろうと降るまいと試合は行われる。
examples.english: The match will be held rain or shine.
examples.pronunciation: ame ga furou to furu mai to shiai ga okonawareru.

examples.japanese: あなたが反対しようとしまいと僕は一人で行くつもりだ。
examples.english: I plan on going alone regardless if you are against it or not.
examples.pronunciation: anata ga hantai shiyou to shimai to boku wa hitori de iku tsumori da.

examples.japanese: 君が信じようが信じまいが、俺は盗んでない。
examples.english: Whether you believe me or not, I didn't steal it.
examples.pronunciation: kimi ga shinji you ga shinji mai ga, ore wa nusundenai.

examples.japanese: 私がいようがいまいが関係なく、彼らはケンカを始めた。
examples.english: Regarless of me being there, they started fighting.
examples.pronunciation: watashi ga i you ga i mai ga kankei naku, karera wa kenka o hajimeta.

examples.japanese: 彼が来ようが来まいが結果は同じだろう。
examples.english: Whether he comes or not, the result will be the same.
examples.pronunciation: kare ga koyou ga komai ga kekka wa onaji darou.

examples.japanese: 気に入ろうと入るまいと、過半数の意見のとおりに決められる。
examples.english: Whether you like it or not, the decision is made by majority.
examples.pronunciation: ki ni irou to iru mai to, kahansuu no iken no toori ni kimerareru.

meaning: even if I wanted to… I couldn’t ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection: V（意向形） + にも + V（ナイ形）ない
usageJapanese: 〜しようとしてもできない / 〜したいと思っても（理由があって）できない。

examples.japanese: 風が強すぎて走ろうにも走れない。
examples.english: The wind was so strong that even if I tried to run, I couldn't.
examples.pronunciation: kaze ga tsuyo sugite hashirou nimo hashirenai.

examples.japanese: 結婚しようにも相手がいない。
examples.english: Even if I wanted to get married, I don't have someone to marry.
examples.pronunciation: kekkon shiyou nimo aite ga ainai.

examples.japanese: 天ぷらを作るにも作り方がわからない。
examples.english: I want to make tempura, but don't know how to make it.
examples.pronunciation: tenpura o tsukuru nimo tsukurikata ga wakaranai.

examples.japanese: 時間がないので友達と遊びに行こうにも行けない。
examples.english: Because I don't have time, even if I want to go out with my friends, I can't.
examples.pronunciation: jikan ga nai node tomodachi to asobi ni ikou nimo ikenai.

examples.japanese: 最終電車が行ってしまって、タクシーに乗ろうにもお金が足りない。
examples.english: The last train is gone and even if I wanted to take a taxi, I don't have enough money.
examples.pronunciation: saishuu densha ga itte shimatte, takushii ni norou nimo okane ga tarinai.

examples.japanese: 日本語の漢字は複雑すぎて、覚えようにも覚えられない。
examples.english: The kanji in Japanese is too complicated, I couldn't remember it even if I wanted to.
examples.pronunciation: nihongo no kanji wa fukuzatsu sugite, oboeyou nimo oboerarenai.

examples.japanese: 電車が止まっているので、会社に行こうにも行けない。
examples.english: Since the trains are stopped, I couldn't go to the office even if I wanted to.
examples.pronunciation: densha ga tomatte iru node, kaisha ni ikou nimo ikenai.

examples.japanese: 警察官なのに振り込め詐欺にあって、泣くにも泣けない。
examples.english: Even though I'm a police officer, I was the victim of a money transfer scam. I can't cry even if I wanted to.
examples.pronunciation: keisatsukan na noni furikome sagi ni atte, naku nimo nakenai.

id: grammar:n1:ようによっては / ようでは
word: ようによっては / ようでは
meaning: depending on the way; depending on how; according to how ~
notes:
・everything else 1 ・ everything else 2
・Verb ・Adv ・Noun ・NaAdj ・IAdj
connection:
V（意向形） + ようによっては
V（意向形） + ようでは
connectionSourcePoint: ようによっては / ようでは

examples.japanese: この絵は見ようによっては男性にも見えるし、女性にも見えます。
examples.english: Depending on how you look at this picture, it looks like a man or a woman.
examples.pronunciation: kono e wa mi you ni yotte wa dansei ni mo mieru shi, josei ni mo miemasu.

examples.japanese: やりようによっては、その仕事は簡単に済ませることができる。
examples.english: That job can be done easily depending on how you do it.
examples.pronunciation: yari you ni yotte wa, sono shigoto wa kantan ni sumaseru koto ga dekiru.

examples.japanese: 見ようでは善にも悪にもなる。
examples.english: Depending on the way you look, there are good things and bad things.
examples.pronunciation: Miyoude wa zen ni mo aku ni mo naru.

examples.japanese: ここから目的地まで、道の混みようによっては１時間かかる場合もある。
examples.english: It may take an hour from here to the destination depending on how busy the road is.
examples.pronunciation: koko kara mokutekichi made, michi no komi you ni yotte wa 1 jikan kakaru baai mo aru.

examples.japanese: どんな道具も使いようによっては便利にも危険にもなります。
examples.english: Regardless of the tool, depending on how it is used can be useful or dangerous.
examples.pronunciation: donna dougu mo tsukai you ni yotte wa benri ni mo kiken ni mo narimasu.

examples.japanese: 相手が強豪チームだとしても、やりようによっては勝てないわけではない。
examples.english: Even if the opponent is a strong team, it does not mean that you cannot win depending on how you do it.
examples.pronunciation: aite ga kyougou chiimu da to shitemo, yari you ni yotte wa katenai wake dewa nai.

examples.japanese: あの山は、見ようによっては仏像が寝ているように見える。
examples.english: That mountain, depending on how you look at it, can be seen as a reclining Buddha image.
examples.pronunciation: ano yama wa, mi you ni yotte wa butsuzou ga nete iru you ni mieru.

examples.japanese: Facebookは便利だが使いようによっては誰かを傷つけてしまうこともある。
examples.english: Facebook is convenient, but it can hurt someone depending on how you use it.
examples.pronunciation: feisubukku wa benri da ga tsukai you ni yotte wa dare ka o kizu tsukete shimau koto mo aru.

meaning: feminine sentence ending particle
notes:
